channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Vans constantly parking on double yellow lines infront of bike shed, 21st June
item.description:
Vans constantly parking on double yellow lines infront of bike shed. As no traffic wardens pass this keeps happening and is dangerous as pedestrians cannot be seen by oncoming vehicles as they approach the end of the road. This is a busy pedestrian route and with school children and parking like this is dangerous. I have witness a few near collisions

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Phoebeth Road, Ladywell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Please clean graffiti, 21st June
item.description:
Please clean graffiti on the Railway Footbridge Queensway/Petts Wood Rd, this one is on the entrance at the Queensway end of the bridge.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queensway, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Obstruction to payment, 21st June
item.description:
Unclear where this has appeared from but needs to be removed

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Glyn Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Missing island box, 21st June
item.description:
Missing island box, cars driving through it are getting damaged
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti needs cleaning on pedestrian footbridge., 21st June
item.description:
Please clean these 3 instances of graffiti on Railway Footbridge Queensway/Petts Wood Rd

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Petts Wood Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hi Viz cone missing from traffic island, 21st June
item.description:
There is a traffic island with a high Viz cone missing from it after a driver drove right into it. Since then, several drivers have hit the island causing damage to their car and potentially dangerous for nearby cars and pedestrians. Issue has occurred on several occasions, the road narrows, is on a bend and has an island. I don't know who in the council approved this layout but it's ridiculous and needs to be re-assessed, by having the island there it's actually made the road and driving more dangerous.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbey Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocking fire access and driveways, 21st June
item.description:
Access to 4 garages blocked and emergency access blocked.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kyverdale Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glass smashed, 21st June
item.description:
Glass smashed

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hazel Road, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Damage - smashed glass or panels / jagged metal edges on shelter

item.title: Fly tipping fridge and furniture, 21st June
item.description:
Fly tipping fridge and furniture
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Rectory Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 4 inch circular hole on drain cover, 21st June
item.description:
4 inch hole on drain cover just rusted through but now a danger to children, dogs or adults as the hole is rusty and very weak as the metal is thin. Very easy for a small child’s leg to go through the cover and has sharp edges.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oaklands Avenue, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large number of refuse bags dumped on corner of Gatonby and kelly Ave, 21st June
item.description:
Large number of refuse bags dumped on corner of Gatonby and Kelly Avenue. Please remove before foxes rip it apart and the trash is spread all over.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39; 41, Kelly Avenue, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish from BBQ, 21st June
item.description:
Some hooligans have used one of the benches by the play area in Grassmead Recreation Ground as their own personal dumping ground after using portable BBQs. As you can see from the photos the rubbish is absolutely everywhere and tomato ketchup squirted everywhere and no one can enjoy the bench! I for one have been using it a lot after recovering from an op. It's become my daily mission to walk to that bench, sadly though that's gone out of the window until the mess is cleared up. I hope it can be cleared ASAP especially in this heat! And people's dogs and other animals will definitely take a liking to it!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stanton Close, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Absndoned bike, 21st June
item.description:
Tipped over and annoying.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Windmill Drive, Clapham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree branches falling off needs pruning, 21st June
item.description:
Please can you come and prune this tree back that overhang inside my garden. As you can see it has dangerous branches that have been falling into my garden. The branches in the pictures are very big and would hurt myself or my family if they fall on our heads. Also the base of the tree is very overgrown which is blocking pathway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 70-72, Elmers End Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large bush with some very long upward branches blowing around and obscuring the green traffic lights g, 21st June
item.description:
When driving down A20 slip road after exiting B&Q taking the left hand lane to turn left into Cray Road their are branches blowing around the traffic light making it difficult n a bright day to see the green light. the once on the roundabout bearing left into Cray road another bush of the same type is wide enough over the road to cause a driver to move suddenly to the right into others coming straight over the lights also driving from Sevenoaks way round the same rounderbout to go straight into Cray road the same set of lights but ones facing the Sevenoaks way direction has the same bush with branches obstruction the green light
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crittall's Corner, North Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Across from 45 Hartland road NW6, 21st June
item.description:
Big pile of broken furniture.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Honiton Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load

item.title: Outside 65/63 Judd Street wC1, 21st June
item.description:
This is a serious trip hazard that older people have already tripped on.It has been there for at least 6 months and nothing has been done about it, Lots of older people live in this area and somebody is going to get seriously injured.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 114, Judd Street, King's Cross, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bin always overflowing, 21st June
item.description:
Bin overflows every day. Fills pavement and green behind with litter daily. Please please just give us a proper double bin with a lid.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dunnock Mews, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vousden Grove - damaged sign, 21st June
item.description:
Street name plate damaged and bent. Unstable now and wobbling out of ground. Is a Royal Greenwich logo’s street name plate.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Packer Place, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Water leak from building, 21st June
item.description:
Water has been leaking from this building which is causing the pavement to be wet and dirty with vegetation starting to grow

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Casimir Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
